channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: What happened to the Jewish left?
item.description: Marc Lamont Hill speaks to Molly Crabapple about the Jewish Labour Bund, anti-Zionism and the Jewish left today.

item.title: Iraqi-born singer Emmanuel Kelly among World Cup half-time show headliners
item.description: Abandoned as a baby and brought up by nuns in Baghdad, Kelly will be preforming alongside global megastars.

item.title: Syria’s Al-Sharaa reshuffles security and intelligence leadership
item.description: Government says the shake-up will strengthen security and clear the way for reconstruction.

item.title: Israeli settlers torch homes and mosque in Masafer Yatta
item.description: Videos show Israeli settlers attacking the Palestinian village of al Tuwani in Masafer Yatta, south of Hebron.

item.title: 5.6 magnitude earthquake hits Peru
item.description: Footage shows firefighters looking for a missing person amongst the rubble of a collapsed building in Pumpunya, Peru.

item.title: Israel uses law to destroy Palestinian education in East Jerusalem
item.description: Legislation recently passed by the Knesset is meant to dismantle the Palestinian education system in East Jerusalem.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins Belgian Grand Prix to extend F1 championship lead
item.description: Mercedes driver won his sixth GP of 2026 as championship rival and teammate George Russell crashes out on first lap.

item.title: Designing cities for a hotter future
item.description: Climate advocate Mina Hasman’s vision of designing cities that behave like forests and buildings that act like trees.

item.title: Tate brothers arrested in the US
item.description: Video shows the moment controversial influencer brothers Andrew Tate and Tristan Tate were arrested in Miami, US.

item.title: Spain vs Argentina LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 final
item.description: Live coverage and text updates as Spain play Argentina in the final of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Why is Argentina’s football team so white?
item.description: Argentina prepares to face Spain in the World Cup final.

item.title: Iran attacks US Gulf allies, causing a fire at Kuwait power plant
item.description: Kuwait and Bahrain report several rounds of Iranian missile, drone attacks; sirens reportedly sound in Jordan.

item.title: Who controls football in Palestine?
item.description: For Palestinians fighting Israeli occupation, football has never been just a game.

item.title: The story behind football stars Messi and Yamal’s viral photo
item.description: The story behind football stars Messi and Yamal’s viral photo

item.title: Why is China protesting about the nationalisation of British Steel?
item.description: The UK move to take over British Steel fuels a brewing dispute with China amid political leadership change in London.

item.title: Lebanon convicts influencer of smuggling drugs in biscuits
item.description: Lebanese food influencer Georges Dib, known as Dr Food, has been sentenced in absentia to life with hard labour.

item.title: Why were Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested? Will they be extradited to UK?
item.description: Influencer brothers arrested in Miami, as UK seeks extradition to face new charges of rape, sex trafficking and assault.

item.title: Why have India’s Gen Z protesters called for a march to parliament?
item.description: After a prominent activist on hunger strike is forcibly removed, organisers announce a march to parliament.

item.title: Indian court rejects bid to release hunger-striking activist from hospital
item.description: The wife of 59-year-old Sonam Wangchuk says his enforced hospitalisation is 'illegal detention'.

item.title: Spain or Argentina, who will win the FIFA World Cup? Ex-player predictions
item.description: Former player predictions as the European and South American champions clash in a World Cup final for the first time.

item.title: India’s cockroach politics is no joke
item.description: The Cockroach Janta Party has crept onto the political scene at a time when Indian democracy is struggling.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Tobin Heath on record goals, France and Lamine Yamal
item.description: In an exclusive interview, Tobin Heath shares her insights before World Cup final and discusses best players and teams.

item.title: Guyana ferry with 116 people on board capsizes; more than 50 rescued
item.description: Rescue operations for remaining passengers and crew ongoing, authorities say.

item.title: Iranians suffer from power blackouts during war and heatwave
item.description: Collapsing grid, battered by military strikes and chronic underinvestment, leaves Iranians sweltering in the dark.

item.title: Why is India vetting books in Kashmir in search of ‘anti-national’ content?
item.description: Authorities in disputed region are auditing school, college and public libraries to look for ‘objectionable’ material.
